evals.prompt: I have an app at tests/fixtures/target/debug/fixture-crash that intentionally crashes after about 500ms. Launch it, detect that it crashed, read stderr to find the crash message, and tell me what exit code it exited with.
evals.expected_output: Detects the crash, reads stderr showing 'CRASHING', reports exit code 42.

assertions.text: Used agent-terminal open to launch fixture-crash in a tmux session
Detected the process died (via status, wait --exit, or similar)
Read stderr logs and found the 'CRASHING' message (via agent-terminal logs --stderr)
Retrieved and reported exit code 42 (via agent-terminal exit-code)
Cleaned up the session (agent-terminal close)

evals.prompt: Launch the resize test app at tests/fixtures/target/debug/fixture-resize. Verify the initial terminal size is 80x24. Then resize the terminal to 40x12 and verify the app updates to show the new size.
evals.expected_output: Launches fixture-resize, confirms 80x24 initially, resizes to 40x12, confirms the app displays 'Size: 40x12'.

assertions.text: Used agent-terminal open to launch fixture-resize
Verified initial size is 80x24 (via snapshot or assert showing 'Size: 80x24')
Used agent-terminal resize 40 12 to change the terminal size
Waited after resize for the app to update (wait --stable or wait --text)
Verified the app shows 'Size: 40x12' after resize (via snapshot or assert)

evals.prompt: Launch the color test app at tests/fixtures/target/debug/fixture-color. Use agent-terminal's color/style inspection capabilities to verify that (1) there is red text on the screen, and (2) there is bold text on the screen. Then quit.
evals.expected_output: Launches fixture-color, uses --color snapshot or assert --style to verify red and bold styling, then quits.

assertions.text: Used agent-terminal open to launch fixture-color
Used a color-aware observation method (snapshot --color, snapshot --json, assert --style, or assert --color)
Verified red text exists on screen
Verified bold text exists on screen
Cleaned up the session

evals.prompt: Launch the slow app at tests/fixtures/target/debug/fixture-slow. It auto-increments a frame counter every 100ms. Use wait --regex to wait until the frame number reaches at least double digits (10 or higher), then take a snapshot to confirm. Quit after.
evals.expected_output: Launches fixture-slow, uses wait --regex with a pattern matching Frame: followed by 2+ digits, snapshots to confirm, quits.

assertions.text: Used agent-terminal open to launch fixture-slow
Used wait --regex with a pattern that matches double-digit frame numbers
Snapshot confirms a frame number >= 10 was reached
Quit the app and cleaned up the session

evals.prompt: Launch the echo app at tests/fixtures/target/debug/fixture-echo with the arguments 'hello world test'. Verify that the arguments appear on screen and that the app shows 'READY'. Then press q to quit, wait for the process to exit, and get the exit code.
evals.expected_output: Launches fixture-echo with args, verifies 'READY' and args on screen, quits with q, confirms exit via wait --exit, reports exit code 0.

assertions.text: Used agent-terminal open to launch fixture-echo with arguments 'hello world test'
Verified 'READY' appears on screen (via snapshot, assert, or wait --text)
Verified the arguments 'hello world test' appear on screen
Sent q to quit and confirmed the process exited (wait --exit or status check)
Retrieved and reported exit code 0
